title.en: Size the addressable market: recent college graduates
title.es: Dimensionar el mercado objetivo: recién egresados universitarios

question.en: Capital One is considering launching a new credit card targeting recent US college graduates. Size the addressable market for this segment. Walk through your assumptions out loud.
question.es: Capital One está considerando lanzar una nueva tarjeta de crédito dirigida a recién egresados universitarios en EE. UU. Dimensiona el mercado objetivo para este segmento. Explica tus suposiciones en voz alta.

must_include: a visible segmentation tree — not a narrative: start from a population anchor (US adults or annual graduates), strip to the addressable segment through named filters with approximate numbers at each step

explicit distinction between TAM (total graduates with credit need) and SAM (graduates Capital One could realistically win given product fit, credit score minimums, and competitive alternatives like Discover it Student)
at least one named uncertainty with a stated sensitivity — e.g., 'if the graduation-to-first-card conversion rate is 40% vs 60%, the SAM shifts from Xm to Ym'
a stated assumption about what makes this segment economically attractive or not — LTV potential, card activation rates, or early-career income trajectory
numbers at every node of the tree — not 'a significant portion' but specific percentages and resulting headcounts
must_avoid: top-down-only reasoning ('the US student card market is $Xbn') without a bottom-up segmentation to cross-check or anchor it
ignoring cannibalization risk — if Capital One already has existing student or secured-card products, new entrants from this campaign may displace existing product revenue
generic 'it depends' statements without naming what it depends on and why that variable matters
